 622 | /// Build tool-calling instructions to inject into the system prompt for models
 623 | /// that don't support native structured tool calls (e.g. gemini-web).
 624 | /// Instructs the model to output `<tool_call>` XML blocks with JSON payloads.
 625 | fn inject_tool_prompt(base_prompt: &str, tools: &[ToolDefinition]) -> String {
 626 |     let tool_lines: String = tools
 627 |         .iter()
 628 |         .map(|t| format!("- {}: {}", t.name, t.description))
 629 |         .collect::<Vec<_>>()
 630 |         .join("\n");
 631 |     format!(
 632 |         "{base_prompt}\n\n\
 633 |          # Tool Use\n\
 634 |          You have access to the following tools. To use a tool, output a \
 635 |          <tool_call> XML block with a JSON object containing \"name\" and \
 636 |          \"arguments\" fields. You may output multiple tool calls in one response.\n\n\
 637 |          Example:\n\
 638 |          <tool_call>\n\
 639 |          {{\"name\": \"bash\", \"arguments\": {{\"command\": \"ls /tmp\"}}}}\n\
 640 |          </tool_call>\n\n\
 641 |          Available tools:\n{tool_lines}\n\n\
 642 |          RULES:\n\
 643 |          1. To use a tool, output <tool_call> blocks. Do NOT describe or \
 644 |          simulate tool usage in plain text. Do NOT fabricate tool output.\n\
 645 |          2. After a tool result is returned, review the result and provide \
 646 |          your final answer in plain text WITHOUT any <tool_call> blocks. \
 647 |          Only call another tool if the result was insufficient.\n\
 648 |          3. Do NOT call the same tool with the same arguments more than once.\n\
 649 |          4. Prefer the lsp tool for code intelligence (symbols/definitions/references). \
 650 |          Prefer the bash tool for shell commands.\n\
 651 |          5. During refactors, NEVER create placeholder/stub implementations \
 652 |          (e.g., TODO, FIXME, \"not implemented\", \"fallback\"). Always preserve \
 653 |          concrete behavior."
 654 |     )
 655 | }
 656 | 
 657 | fn list_tools_bootstrap_definition() -> ToolDefinition {
 658 |     ToolDefinition {
 659 |         name: "list_tools".to_string(),

content.text: I’m updating the session/TUI pipeline so proactive LSP diagnostics are always injected up front for TUI chats, instead of being optional or limited to Gemini Web. Then I’ll run formatting and targeted tests/build checks.

content.content: src/session/mod.rs:3283: pub enum SessionEvent {

3278 |     pub session_id: String,
3279 | }
3280 | 
3281 | /// Events emitted during session processing for real-time UI updates
3282 | #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
3283 | pub enum SessionEvent {
3284 |     /// Agent is thinking/processing
3285 |     Thinking,
3286 |     /// Tool call started
3287 |     ToolCallStart { name: String, arguments: String },
3288 |     /// Tool call completed with result
3289 |     ToolCallComplete {
3290 |         name: String,
3291 |         output: String,
3292 |         success: bool,
3293 |     },
3294 |     /// Partial text output (for streaming)
3295 |     TextChunk(String),
3296 |     /// Final text output
3297 |     TextComplete(String),
3298 |     /// Model thinking/reasoning output
3299 |     ThinkingComplete(String),
3300 |     /// Token usage for one LLM round-trip
3301 |     UsageReport {
3302 |         prompt_tokens: usize,
3303 |         completion_tokens: usize,
3304 |         duration_ms: u64,
3305 |         model: String,
3306 |     },
3307 |     /// Updated session state for caller to sync back
3308 |     SessionSync(Box<Session>),
3309 |     /// Processing complete
3310 |     Done,
3311 |     /// Error occurred
3312 |     Error(String),
3313 | }
3314 | 
3315 | /// List all sessions
3316 | pub async fn list_sessions() -> Result<Vec<SessionSummary>> {
3317 |     let sessions_dir = crate::config::Config::data_dir()
3318 |         .map(|d| d.join("sessions"))
3319 |         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("Could not determine data directory"))?;
3320 | 
3321 |     if !sessions_dir.exists() {
3322 |         return Ok(Vec::new());
3323 |     }
3324 | 
3325 |     let mut summaries = Vec::new();
3326 |     let mut entries = fs::read_dir(&sessions_dir).await?;
3327 | 
3328 |     while let Some(entry) = entries.next_entry().await? {
3329 |         let path = entry.path();
3330 |         if path.extension().map(|e| e == "json").unwrap_or(false)
3331 |             && let Ok(content) = fs::read_to_string(&path).await
3332 |             && let Ok(session) = serde_json::from_str::<Session>(&content)
3333 |         {
3334 |             summaries.push(SessionSummary {
3335 |                 id: session.id,
3336 |                 title: session.title,
3337 |                 created_at: session.created_at,
3338 |                 updated_at: session.updated_at,
3339 |                 message_count: session.messages.len(),
3340 |                 agent: session.agent,
3341 |                 directory: session.metadata.directory,
3342 |             });
3343 |         }
3344 |     }
3345 | 
3346 |     summaries.sort_by(|a, b| b.updated_at.cmp(&a.updated_at));
3347 |     Ok(summaries)

content.text: I found the proactive LSP injection is implemented in `src/session/mod.rs`, but it’s currently gated to `gemini-web`, step 1 only, and it fetches `documentSymbol` rather than actual LSP diagnostics/errors. I’m going to inspect the internal LSP manager/client to see whether diagnostics already exist, and if not, I’ll wire a lightweight diagnostics snapshot into the proactive context path so TUI sessions always include real LSP errors before the model responds.

1 | //! LSP (Language Server Protocol) client implementation
   2 | //!
   3 | //! Provides a complete LSP client for code intelligence features:
   4 | //! - Go to definition
   5 | //! - Find references
   6 | //! - Hover information
   7 | //! - Document symbols
   8 | //! - Workspace symbols
   9 | //! - Code completion
  10 | //!
  11 | //! Supports language servers via stdio transport with JSON-RPC 2.0.
  12 | 
  13 | pub mod client;
  14 | pub mod transport;
  15 | pub mod types;
  16 | 
  17 | pub use client::LspManager;
  18 | pub use types::*;

1 | //! LSP type definitions based on LSP 3.17 specification
   2 | //!
   3 | //! These types map directly to the LSP protocol types.
   4 | 
   5 | use anyhow::Result;
   6 | use lsp_types::{

   8 |     ServerCapabilities, SymbolInformation, TextDocumentIdentifier, TextDocumentItem,
   9 | };
  10 | use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
  11 | use serde_json::Value;
  12 | use tracing::{info, warn};
  13 | 
  14 | /// LSP client configuration
  15 | #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
  16 | pub struct LspConfig {
  17 |     /// Command to spawn the language server
  18 |     pub command: String,
  19 |     /// Arguments to pass to the language server
  20 |     #[serde(default)]
  21 |     pub args: Vec<String>,
  22 |     /// Root URI for the workspace
  23 |     pub root_uri: Option<String>,
  24 |     /// File extensions this server handles
  25 |     #[serde(default)]
  26 |     pub file_extensions: Vec<String>,
  27 |     /// Initialization options to pass to the server
  28 |     #[serde(default)]
  29 |     pub initialization_options: Option<Value>,
  30 |     /// Timeout for requests in milliseconds
  31 |     #[serde(default = "default_timeout")]
  32 |     pub timeout_ms: u64,
  33 | }
  34 | 
  35 | fn default_timeout() -> u64 {
  36 |     30000
  37 | }
  38 | 
  39 | fn rust_timeout() -> u64 {
  40 |     120000
  41 | }
  42 | 
  43 | impl Default for LspConfig {
  44 |     fn default() -> Self {
  45 |         Self {
  46 |             command: String::new(),
  47 |             args: Vec::new(),
  48 |             root_uri: None,
  49 |             file_extensions: Vec::new(),
  50 |             initialization_options: None,
  51 |             timeout_ms: default_timeout(),
  52 |         }
  53 |     }
  54 | }
  55 | 
  56 | /// JSON-RPC request for LSP
  57 | #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
  58 | pub struct JsonRpcRequest {
  59 |     pub jsonrpc: String,
  60 |     pub id: i64,
  61 |     pub method: String,
  62 |     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
  63 |     pub params: Option<Value>,
  64 | }
  65 | 
  66 | impl JsonRpcRequest {
  67 |     pub fn new(id: i64, method: &str, params: Option<Value>) -> Self {
  68 |         Self {
  69 |             jsonrpc: "2.0".to_string(),
  70 |             id,
  71 |             method: method.to_string(),
  72 |             params,
  73 |         }
  74 |     }
  75 | }
  76 | 
  77 | /// JSON-RPC response for LSP
  78 | #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
  79 | pub struct JsonRpcResponse {
  80 |     pub jsonrpc: String,
  81 |     pub id: i64,
  82 |     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
  83 |     pub result: Option<Value>,
  84 |     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
  85 |     pub error: Option<JsonRpcError>,
  86 | }
  87 | 
  88 | /// JSON-RPC error

  93 |     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]

  95 | }
  96 | 
  97 | /// JSON-RPC notification for LSP

content.text: The current LSP transport doesn’t track server-pushed diagnostics at all, so “real LSP errors” can’t currently be injected. I’m going to add diagnostics capture at the transport/client layer, expose a diagnostics query on the LSP tool/client, and then replace the TUI proactive LSP prefetch so it always injects diagnostics-first context for step 1 instead of only optional document symbols.

content.content:

src/tui/mod.rs:5533: .prompt_with_events(&msg_clone, tx.clone(), registry)

content.text: I’ve confirmed the TUI goes through `prompt_with_events`, so the right place to enforce this is the shared session pipeline, not TUI rendering. I’m implementing it there so the TUI immediately receives proactive LSP diagnostics as a system message/tool context before the first model completion.

content.text:
Implemented.

What changed:
- Added real LSP diagnostics capture to the LSP transport/client stack:
  - `src/lsp/transport.rs`
  - `src/lsp/client.rs`
  - `src/lsp/types.rs`
- Added a new `lsp` action:
  - `action: "diagnostics"`
  - formatted in `src/tool/lsp.rs`
- Changed proactive TUI/session LSP injection so it is no longer Gemini-only or “optional-first”:
  - `src/session/mod.rs`
  - now injects mandatory proactive LSP context on step 1 for detected files
  - diagnostics are preferred
  - document symbols are only used if diagnostics are empty

Behavior now:
- In TUI chat flow, before the first model reply, the session injects a system message like:
  - “Mandatory proactive LSP context… Prioritize these real LSP diagnostics and errors over speculation…”
- This gives the model actual LSP-reported errors immediately, instead of hoping it decides to call the `lsp` tool itself.

Notes:
- The repo currently has unrelated compile failures in `src/a2a/worker.rs` (`crate::forage` missing), so full `cargo check` / tests don’t complete cleanly.
- `cargo fmt` was run.
- The LSP changes themselves are in place in the files above.

If you want, next I can also make the TUI visibly render this prefetched diagnostics block as a distinct “LSP errors” panel/banner before the assistant response, instead of only injecting it into model context.
